steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The subject is the singular noun phrase 'the word "stockade"'. The sentence is parallel with '"cinch" derives from cincho,' so the blank needs a third-person singular simple-present verb.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — have derived: Present perfect plural — wrong number and wrong tense for parallelism with 'derives'. ✗
  • Choice B — were deriving: Past progressive plural — wrong number and wrong tense. ✗
  • Choice C — derive: Base form / plural present — does not agree with the singular subject 'the word "stockade"'. ✗
  • Choice D — derives: Third-person singular present — agrees with the singular subject and parallels 'derives' in the coordinated clause. ✓
That is the correct answer.
